articles.title: Soccer-Scotland aim to end first-round World Cup hoodoo
articles.description: June 1 (Reuters) - Scotland and ⁠their raucous Tartan Army of fans head to their first World Cup since 1998 determined to ⁠reach the knockout phase of a major tournament for the first time after glorious but ‌heartbreaking near-misses in the past.

articles.title: 😳Alarm in France over Saliba, he could be out for weeks
articles.description: According to reports from France, the Arsenal centre-back is dealing with an injury that may have worsened and could put his participation in the World Cup at risk. Maximum caution and concern over William Saliba in France, as reports from the country suggest the player’s injury worsened last Saturday after the Champions League final, and there is now a risk that he may miss the World Cup. If this issue is confirmed, it would be a very hard blow for Deschamps, as France make their World Cup debut on June 16 against Senegal. All eyes are on Saliba. articles.title: French Open fines Vallejo $76K for sexist remark
articles.description: Paraguay's Adolfo Daniel Vallejo has been ordered to pay half his French Open prize money, roughly $76,000, for saying his second-round match should not have been umpired by a woman.

articles.title: Soccer-Mexico's World Cup 'curse' faces ultimate test on home soil
